preamble: The transformation from an alveolar trill ([r]) to a voiced dental fricative ([ð]) is a documented phonetic shift, primarily observed as a form of lenition where a trill weakens to a fricative. This change is a notable characteristic of certain dialects, distinguishing them from related languages.
phoneticEffects: Lenition (trill to fricative), slight place assimilation (alveolar to dental)
sources: Sallabank, J. (2011). Norman Languages of the Channel Islands. Shima: The International Journal of Research into Island Cultures, 5(2), 37-52.
Jones, M. C. (2015). Variation and Change in Mainland and Insular Norman: A study of superstrate influence. Brill.
